content.full_text:
October 21, 1976

Jim Graham  
California Manufacturers Association  
P.O. Box 1138  
Sacramento, CA 95805

Dear Mr. Graham:

This letter confirms our telephone conversation of October 19, 1976, concerning a campaign statement filed by the CMA's political action committee for the period September 21, 1976, through October 18, 1976. The political action committee made monetary contributions to approximately 80 legislative candidates. Most of the contributions were $100, although there was some variation in the amount contributed. In addition, the committee incurred approximately $900 in overhead expenses. You asked how to complete the first page of the Form 420, which requires an allocation of expenditures and contributions by candidates and measures.

As I stated in that conversation, you must disclose the relevant information, even though such disclosure repeats, almost verbatim, the information contained on Schedule E, and necessitates the inclusion of five or six extra pages in your campaign statement. However, it is not necessary to attribute a portion of the overhead expenses to each candidate. Instead you should make a notation on the "Allocation" indicating that approximately $900 in overhead costs were incurred. Such a notation will be sufficient to indicate that all candidates benefited to some extent from the costs incurred.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West  
Staff Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: How should a political action committee allocate and disclose contributions to approximately 80 legislative candidates and overhead expenses on Form 420 campaign statement disclosures?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The PAC must disclose all relevant contribution and expenditure information on the allocation section of Form 420, even if it duplicates Schedule E information, but may note overhead expenses in aggregate rather than allocating them to individual candidates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from letter body. Confirmation of telephone conversation on campaign finance disclosure requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should a political action committee allocate and disclose contributions to approximately 80 legislative candidates and overhead expenses on Form 420 campaign statement disclosures?

CONCLUSION: The PAC must disclose all relevant contribution and expenditure information on the allocation section of Form 420, even if it duplicates Schedule E information, but may note overhead expenses in aggregate rather than allocating them to individual candidates.
